generatedSentence: An anonymous former visionary, Silas, now an aging security consultant, felt his career was lagging. His days were spent in maintenance for a rickety hotel in the northern territories, a far cry from his happiest moments. He often indulged in a simple aliment, a banana, for a brief lunch, and tried to forgive himself for past mistakes. His smartphone was a constant reminder of the outside web, a universal network he once helped secure. He felt an unpretending, outsize lack, a bad feeling, considering his possible potential. He sometimes had flashbacks to a time he felt understood. His local life was far from any story he'd envisioned. One night, checking a storage unit's security feeds, Silas saw something terrible: a warlord figure, surrounded by snakes tattoos, discussing an offensive operation. This nutcase was planning an insurrection, a truly vicious theme. Silas felt a surge of empathy for the innocent, overriding his usual hardline stance. He knew this wasn't some elementary threat; it was a hazard that made his nostrils flare with a strange odor of fear. The walls of his small office felt oppressive as he realized he might be the only one to acknowledge the looming danger, no longer cherrypicking facts. A parallel track opened in his mind, pulling him like a magnet. This was no daydream; he had to grow. Feeling finally rested, Silas donned his old jumper. He knew the vital importance of this construction site confrontation, a secret under a non-disclosure agreement. His teammate, a retired cook with a knack for mathematics, provided nodal intel. He'd seen reports of the enemy, almost like scarecrow figures, guarding a derrick on the highway, their faces slick with vaseline. He couldn't underestimate their ferocity. His mind raced, a furious multitasking he hadn't employed in years, picturing a velociraptor-like surge of enemy vehicles, fueled by octane, like a rushing stream of destruction. This was an interim strike, a desperate gamble. He had to stomp out the threat before it truly caught fire. A sense of wonder mixed with dread as he prepared for the gradients of danger, feeling the cold touch of a potential hangman's fate. The dust settled. Silas, though alienated from the conventional world, felt a sense of completion. His evaluation of the threat had been accurate. The warlord's forces were deactivated, their dark reign ending, as if pulled underwater. He found a strange restorative peace, a halo around his weary head. His efforts, though not under the solar spotlight, brought light, freeing him from the unseen moderators of his past. He considered the future, not as a vagrant or a vampire of old skills, but as someone who had made a difference. He didn't need a receipt or a telephone call of thanks. He merely allowed himself to nestle into a worn velvet chair, picking up a forgotten fortepiano sheet music. He counted his few nickels in a small jar, noting the lard and fruit he still had. He was no longer a baby in this fight. Even the thought of a walrus with a denture made him smile. The insect of doubt was gone.
panelSentences: An anonymous former visionary, Silas, now an aging security consultant, felt his career was lagging. His days were spent in maintenance for a rickety hotel in the northern territories, a far cry from his happiest moments. He often indulged in a simple aliment, a banana, for a brief lunch, and tried to forgive himself for past mistakes. His smartphone was a constant reminder of the outside web, a universal network he once helped secure. He felt an unpretending, outsize lack, a bad feeling, considering his possible potential. He sometimes had flashbacks to a time he felt understood. His local life was far from any story he'd envisioned.

One night, checking a storage unit's security feeds, Silas saw something terrible: a warlord figure, surrounded by snakes tattoos, discussing an offensive operation. This nutcase was planning an insurrection, a truly vicious theme. Silas felt a surge of empathy for the innocent, overriding his usual hardline stance. He knew this wasn't some elementary threat; it was a hazard that made his nostrils flare with a strange odor of fear. The walls of his small office felt oppressive as he realized he might be the only one to acknowledge the looming danger, no longer cherrypicking facts. A parallel track opened in his mind, pulling him like a magnet. This was no daydream; he had to grow.

Feeling finally rested, Silas donned his old jumper. He knew the vital importance of this construction site confrontation, a secret under a non-disclosure agreement. His teammate, a retired cook with a knack for mathematics, provided nodal intel. He'd seen reports of the enemy, almost like scarecrow figures, guarding a derrick on the highway, their faces slick with vaseline. He couldn't underestimate their ferocity. His mind raced, a furious multitasking he hadn't employed in years, picturing a velociraptor-like surge of enemy vehicles, fueled by octane, like a rushing stream of destruction. This was an interim strike, a desperate gamble. He had to stomp out the threat before it truly caught fire. A sense of wonder mixed with dread as he prepared for the gradients of danger, feeling the cold touch of a potential hangman's fate.

The dust settled. Silas, though alienated from the conventional world, felt a sense of completion. His evaluation of the threat had been accurate. The warlord's forces were deactivated, their dark reign ending, as if pulled underwater. He found a strange restorative peace, a halo around his weary head. His efforts, though not under the solar spotlight, brought light, freeing him from the unseen moderators of his past. He considered the future, not as a vagrant or a vampire of old skills, but as someone who had made a difference. He didn't need a receipt or a telephone call of thanks. He merely allowed himself to nestle into a worn velvet chair, picking up a forgotten fortepiano sheet music. He counted his few nickels in a small jar, noting the lard and fruit he still had. He was no longer a baby in this fight. Even the thought of a walrus with a denture made him smile. The insect of doubt was gone.

imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, gritty detective comic art style, thick white gutters, clean black borders. All humans are visibly adult with mature features like facial hair and wrinkles. Cel-shading, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Silas, an aging, grizzled man with a salt-and-pepper beard, slumped at a messy desk, staring at a flickering monitor with a despondent expression, overhead shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: Silas, looking older with deep wrinkles, leaning in close to the monitor, eyes wide with alarm, seeing shadowy figures on a security feed, medium shot, dramatic chiaroscuro lighting. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Silas, a determined adult with clenched jaw, alongside an older, strong-featured female teammate, both moving stealthily through a dark, industrial construction site at night, low angle shot, dramatic backlighting. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Silas, a mature man with a weary but content smile, nestling into a worn velvet armchair, bathed in soft, restorative light filtering through a window, close-up on his face, peaceful expression. No text, no speech bubbles, no dialogue, no children.

definitions.definition: Error, mistake.
An item (or kind of item) of merchandise with negative value; an unwanted good.
Unfavorable; negative; not good.
definitions.example: Abducting children is bad for mothers.

definitions.definition: Not suitable or fitting.
definitions.example: Do you think it is a bad idea to confront him directly?

definitions.definition: Not appropriate, of manners etc.
definitions.example: It is bad manners to talk with your mouth full.

definitions.definition: Unhealthy; liable to cause health problems.
definitions.example: Lard is bad for you. Smoking is bad for you, too. Grapes are bad for dogs but not for humans.

definitions.definition: Sickly, unhealthy, unwell.
definitions.example: I went to the hospital to see how my grandfather was doing. Unfortunately, he's in a bad state.

definitions.definition: Not behaving; behaving badly; misbehaving; mischievous or disobedient.
definitions.example: Stop being bad, or you will get a spanking!

definitions.definition: Tricky; stressful; unpleasant.
definitions.example: Divorce is usually a bad experience for everybody involved.

definitions.definition: Evil; wicked.
definitions.example: Be careful. There are bad people in the world.

definitions.definition: Faulty; not functional.
definitions.example: I had a bad headlight.

definitions.definition: (of food) Spoiled, rotten, overripe.
definitions.example: These apples have gone bad.

definitions.definition: (of breath) Malodorous; foul.
definitions.example: Bad breath is not pleasant for anyone.

definitions.definition: False; counterfeit; illegitimate.
definitions.example: They were caught trying to pass bad coinage.

definitions.definition: Unskilled; of limited ability; not good.
definitions.example: He's a bad gardener; everything he tries to grow ends up dying.

definitions.definition: Of poor physical appearance.
definitions.example: I don't look bad in this dress, do I?

definitions.definition: Bold and daring.
Good, superlative, excellent, cool.
definitions.example: Man, that new car you bought is bad!

definitions.definition: (of a need, want, or pain) Severe, urgent.
definitions.example: He is in bad need of a haircut.

definitions.definition: Overly promiscuous, licentious.
(of a draft/check) Not covered by funds on account.
definitions.example: He gave me a bad check.

definitions.definition: Badly.
definitions.example: I didn't do too bad in the last exam.

definitions.definition: A subject of a talk or an artistic piece; a topic.
A recurring idea; a motif.
An essay written for school.
The main melody of a piece of music, especially one that is the source of variations.
A song, or a snippet of a song, that identifies a film, a TV program, a character, etc. by playing at the appropriate time.
The collection of color schemes, sounds, artwork etc., that "skin" an environment towards a particular motif.
(grammar) The stem of a word.
Thematic relation of a noun phrase to a verb.
Theta role in generative grammar and government and binding theory.
Topic, what is generally being talked about, as opposed to rheme.
A regional unit of organisation in the Byzantine empire.
To give a theme to.
definitions.example: We themed the birthday party around superheroes.

definitions.definition: To apply a theme to; to change the visual appearance and/or layout of (software).
One who is on the same team.
A person who prepares food.
definitions.example: I'm a terrible cook, so I eat a lot of frozen dinners.

definitions.definition: The head cook of a manor house
The degree or quality of cookedness of food
One who manufactures certain illegal drugs, especially meth.
definitions.example: Police found two meth cooks working in the illicit lab.

definitions.definition: A session of manufacturing certain illegal drugs, especially meth.
A fish, the European striped wrasse, Labrus mixtus.
The act or state of being or making something complete; conclusion, accomplishment.
The conclusion of an act of conveyancing concerning the sale of a property.
A forward pass that is successfully caught by the intended receiver.
The act of making a metric space complete by adding points.
The space resulting from such an act.
A sequence of real or fictional events; or, an account of such a sequence.
definitions.example: The book tells the story of two roommates.

definitions.definition: A lie, fiction.
definitions.example: You’ve been telling stories again, haven’t you?

definitions.definition: (usually pluralized) A soap opera.
definitions.example: What will she do without being able to watch her stories?

definitions.definition: History.
A sequence of events, or a situation, such as might be related in an account.
definitions.example: I tried it again; same story, no error message, nothing happened.

definitions.definition: A chronological collection of pictures or short videos published by a user on an application or website that is typically only available for a short period.
To tell as a story; to relate or narrate about.
Food.
Nourishment, sustenance.
An allowance for maintenance; alimony.
To feed, nourish.
To sustain, support.
A wild pig of the genus Phacochoerus, native to Africa.
A nickname for the A-10 Thunderbolt II air support warplane
A high military officer in a warlike nation.
A local ruler or bandit leader usually where the government is weak.
To fail to keep up (the pace), to fall behind
To cover (for example, pipes) with felt strips or similar material (referring to a time lag effect in thermal transfer)
To transport as a punishment for crime.
To cause to lag; to slacken.
The covering of something with strips of felt, wood etc, either as insulation or for protection.
The material so used.
A prison sentence.
Falling behind, not keeping up the pace
Occurring after; indicating the later phase of
Identification with or understanding of the thoughts, feelings, or emotional state of another person.
definitions.example: She had a lot of empathy for her neighbor; she knew what it was like to lose a parent too.

definitions.definition: Capacity to understand another person's point of view or the result of such understanding.
A paranormal ability to psychically read another person's emotions.
MDMA.
An elementary school
(mysticism) A supernatural being which is associated with the elements.
Relating to the basic, essential or fundamental part of something.
Relating to an elementary school.
Relating to a subatomic particle.
Sublunary; not celestial; belonging to the sublunary sphere, to which the four classical elements (earth, air, fire and water) were confined; composed of or pertaining to these four elements.
An attack.
definitions.example: The Marines today launched a major offensive.

definitions.definition: The posture of attacking or being able to attack.
definitions.example: He took the offensive in the press, accusing his opponent of corruption.

definitions.definition: Causing offense; arousing a visceral reaction of disgust, anger, or hatred.
definitions.example: An offensive smell.

definitions.definition: Relating to an offense or attack, as opposed to defensive.
definitions.example: The army's offensive capabilities. An offensive weapon.

definitions.definition: Having to do with play directed at scoring.
definitions.example: The offensive coordinator is responsible for ordering all rushing plays.

definitions.definition: A piece of material that attracts some metals by magnetism.
(preceded by a noun) A person or thing that attracts what is denoted by the preceding noun.
definitions.example: He always had a girl on his arm – he's a bit of a babe magnet.

definitions.definition: Having a feeling arising from a consciousness of well-being or of enjoyment; enjoying good of any kind, such as comfort, peace, or tranquillity; blissful, contented, joyous.
definitions.example: Music makes me feel happy.

definitions.definition: Experiencing the effect of favourable fortune; favored by fortune or luck; fortunate, lucky, propitious.
Content, satisfied (with or to do something); having no objection (to something).
definitions.example: Are you happy to pay me back by the end of the week?

definitions.definition: (Of acts, speech, etc.) Appropriate, apt, felicitous.
definitions.example: a happy coincidence

definitions.definition: (in combination) Favoring or inclined to use.
definitions.example: slaphappy, trigger-happy

definitions.definition: (of people, often followed by "at" or "in") Dexterous, ready, skilful.
Something with restoring properties.
An alcoholic drink, especially with tonic.
Serving to restore.
definitions.example: After a long day working in the fields Clarence took comfort in a restorative pint of beer.

definitions.definition: An arthropod in the class Insecta, characterized by six legs, up to four wings, and a chitinous exoskeleton.
definitions.example: Our shed has several insect infestions, including ants, yellowjackets, and wasps.

definitions.definition: Any small arthropod similar to an insect including spiders, centipedes, millipedes, etc
definitions.example: The swamp is swarming with every sort of insect.

definitions.definition: A contemptible or powerless person.
definitions.example: The manager’s assistant was the worst sort of insect.

definitions.definition: To make something inactive or no longer effective
To prevent the action of a biochemical agent (such as an enzyme)
To remove a person or piece of hardware from active military service
To convey or transfer to another, as title, property, or right; to part voluntarily with ownership of.
To estrange; to withdraw affections or attention from; to make indifferent or averse, where love or friendship before subsisted.
Isolated; excluded; estranged.
Fat from the abdomen of a pig, especially as prepared for use in cooking or pharmacy.
Fatty meat from a pig; bacon, pork.
To stuff (meat) with bacon or pork before cooking.
To smear with fat or lard.
To garnish or strew, especially with reference to words or phrases in speech and writing.
To fatten; to enrich.
To grow fat.
To mix or garnish with something, as by way of improvement; to interlard.

A small agile dinosaur, of the genus Velociraptor, having sickle-shaped claws. It is also thought to have had a feathered coat. Fossils have been found in Late Cretaceous deposits Mongolia and China (around 75-71 mya)
A motor vehicle for transporting large numbers of people along roads.
An electrical conductor or interface serving as a common connection for two or more circuits or components.
(medical industry) An ambulance.
A main, direct public road, especially a multi-lane, high speed thoroughfare connecting major population centers.
Any public road for vehicular traffic.
A circular band of coloured light, visible around the sun or moon etc., caused by reflection and refraction of light by ice crystals in the atmosphere.
A cloud of gas and other matter surrounding and captured by the gravitational field of a large diffuse astronomical object, such as a galaxy or cluster of galaxies.
Anything resembling this band, such as an effect caused by imperfect developing of photographs.
Nimbus, a luminous disc, often of gold, around or over the heads of saints, etc., in religious paintings.
The metaphorical aura of glory, veneration or sentiment which surrounds an idealized entity.
(iconography) a circular annulus ring, frequently luminous, often golden, floating above the head
A circular brace used to keep the head and neck in position.
A rollbar placed in front of the driver, used to protect the cockpit of a open cockpit racecar.
To encircle with a halo.
An elongated curved tropical fruit that grows in bunches and has a creamy flesh and a smooth skin.

The tropical tree-like plant which bears clusters of bananas. The plant, usually of the genus Musa but sometimes also including plants from Ensete, has large, elongated leaves and is related to the plantain.
A yellow colour, like that of a banana's skin.

(mildly) A person of Asian descent, especially a Chinese American, who has assimilated into Western culture or married a Caucasian (from the "yellow" outside and "white" inside). Compare coconut or Oreo.
A banana equivalent dose.
A catamorphism (from the use of banana brackets in the notation).
The penis.
A banana kick.
Curved like a banana, especially of a ball in flight.
An eccentric or odd person.
Someone who is insane.
Of or pertaining to the sun; proceeding from the sun

Measured by the progress or revolution of the sun in the ecliptic; as, the solar year.
Produced by the action of the sun, or peculiarly affected by its influence.
To pardon; to waive any negative feeling or desire for punishment, retribution, or compensation.
definitions.example: Forgive others, not because they deserve forgiveness, but because you deserve peace.

definitions.definition: To accord forgiveness.
A bet or wager.
definitions.example: I had a gamble on the horses last weekend.

A risky venture.
definitions.example: The sailors had taken many gambles with the sea and always won.

definitions.definition: To take a risk, with the potential of a positive outcome.
To play risky games, especially casino games, for monetary gain.
To risk (something) for potential gain.
definitions.example: He gambled his reputation on the outcome.

definitions.definition: To interact with equipment at a casino
A person who lives near a given place.
definitions.example: It's easy to tell the locals from the tourists.

definitions.definition: A branch of a nationwide organization such as a trade union.
definitions.example: I'm in the TWU, too. Local 6.

definitions.definition: A train that stops at all, or almost all, stations between its origin and destination, including very small ones.
definitions.example: The expresses skipped my station, so I had to take a local.

definitions.definition: One's nearest or regularly frequented public house or bar.
definitions.example: I got barred from my local, so I've started going all the way into town for a drink.

definitions.definition: A locally scoped identifier.
definitions.example: Functional programming languages usually don't allow changing the immediate value of locals once they've been initialized, unless they're explicitly marked as being mutable.

definitions.definition: An item of news relating to the place where the newspaper is published.
From or in a nearby location.
definitions.example: We prefer local produce.

definitions.definition: (of a variable or identifier) Having limited scope (either lexical or dynamic); only being accessible within a certain portion of a program.
(of a condition or state) Applying to each point in a space rather than the space as a whole.
Of or pertaining to a restricted part of an organism.
definitions.example: The patient didn't want to be sedated, so we applied only local anesthesia.

definitions.definition: Descended from an indigenous population.
definitions.example: Hawaiian Pidgin is spoken by the local population.

definitions.definition: In the local area; within a city, state, country, etc.
definitions.example: It's never been more important to buy local.

definitions.definition: An anesthetic (anesthetic substance) that causes loss of sensation only to the area to which it is applied.
Someone or something that jumps, e.g. a participant in a jumping event in track or skiing.
A person who attempts suicide by jumping from a great height.
A short length of electrical conductor, to make a temporary connection. Also jump wire.
A removable connecting pin on an electronic circuit board.

A long drilling tool used by masons and quarry workers, consisting of an iron bar with a chisel-edged steel tip at one or both ends, operated by striking it against the rock, turning it slightly with each blow.
A crude kind of sleigh, usually a simple box on runners which are in one piece with the poles that form the thills.
A jumping spider
The larva of the cheese fly.
(18th century) One of certain Calvinistic Methodists in Wales whose worship was characterized by violent convulsions.
A spring to impel the star wheel, or a pawl to lock fast a wheel, in a repeating timepiece.
A shot in which the player releases the ball at the highest point of a jump; a jump shot.
A nuclear power plant worker who repairs equipment in areas with extremely high levels of radiation.
To connect with an electrical jumper.
Someone who has visions; a seer
An impractical dreamer
Someone who has positive ideas about the future
Having vision or foresight
Imaginary or illusory
Prophetic or revelatory
Idealistic or utopian

definitions.definition: To grasp a concept fully and thoroughly, especially (of words, statements, art, etc.) to be aware of the meaning of and (of people) to be aware of the intent of.
definitions.example: I'm sorry. I don't understand.

definitions.definition: To believe, to think one grasps sufficiently despite potentially incomplete knowledge.
definitions.example: I understand that you have a package for me?

definitions.definition: (obsolete outside circus, acrobatics) To stand underneath, to support.

Indicates comprehension on the part of the speaker.
Actions performed to keep some machine or system functioning or in service.
A tort committed when a third party who does not have a bona fide interest in a lawsuit provides help or acquires an interest to a litigant's lawsuit.

Child support.

Of the nature of, or relating to, a node.

definitions.definition: Of or relating to the nodus.

A tall, thin, awkward person.
Anything that appears terrifying but presents no danger.

A bird, the black tern.

(often in the plural) In general, a product of plant growth useful to man or animals.

Specifically, a sweet, edible part of a plant that resembles seed-bearing fruit (see next sense), even if it does not develop from a floral ovary; also used in a technically imprecise sense for some sweet or sweetish vegetables, such as the petioles of rhubarb, that resemble a true fruit or are used in cookery as if they were a fruit.
A product of fertilization in a plant, specifically:
An end result, effect, or consequence; advantageous or disadvantageous result.
definitions.example: His long nights in the office eventually bore fruit when his business boomed and he was given a raise.

definitions.definition: Of, belonging to, related to, or having fruit or its characteristics; (of living things) producing or consuming fruit.
definitions.example: the fresh-squeezed fruit juice; a fruit salad; an artificial fruit flavor; a fruit tree; a fruit bat

definitions.definition: A homosexual man; an effeminate man.
Offspring from a sexual union.
definitions.example: Blessed art thou amongst women and blessed is the fruit of thy womb.

definitions.definition: To produce fruit, seeds, or spores.
Relating to, or characteristic of life.
definitions.example: vital energies; vital functions; vital actions

definitions.definition: Necessary to the continuation of life; being the seat of life; being that on which life depends.
definitions.example: The brain is a vital organ.

definitions.definition: Invigorating or life-giving.
Necessary to continued existence.
definitions.example: The transition to farming was vital for the creation of civilisation.

definitions.definition: Relating to the recording of life events.
definitions.example: Birth, marriage and death certificates are vital records.

definitions.definition: Very important.
definitions.example: It is vital that you don't forget to do your homework.

definitions.definition: Containing life; living.
Capable of living; in a state to live; viable.
A large town house or mansion; a grand private residence, especially in France.
An establishment that provides accommodation and other services for paying guests; normally larger than a guesthouse, and often one of a chain.
A restaurant; any dining establishment.
The letter H in the ICAO spelling alphabet.
The larger red property in the game of Monopoly, in contradistinction to houses.
The guest accommodation and dining section of a cruise ship.
To cease from action, motion, work, or performance of any kind; stop; desist; be without motion.
To come to a pause or an end; end.
To be free from that which harasses or disturbs; be quiet or still; be undisturbed.
To be or to put into a state of rest.
definitions.example: I shall not rest until I have uncovered the truth.

definitions.definition: To stay, remain, be situated.
definitions.example: The blame seems to rest with your father.

definitions.definition: To lean, lie, or lay.
definitions.example: A column rests on its pedestal.

definitions.definition: To complete one's active advocacy in a trial or other proceeding, and thus to wait for the outcome (however, one is still generally available to answer questions, etc.)
definitions.example: I rest my case.

definitions.definition: To sleep; slumber.
To lie dormant.
To sleep the final sleep; sleep in death; die; be dead.
To rely or depend on.

Characteristic of the North of England (usually capitalised)

definitions.definition: The act of storing goods; the state of being stored.

To put into storage; to store.

definitions.definition: Lacking individuality.

definitions.definition: A person who wanders from place to place; a nomad, a wanderer.

At the University of Dublin, either the first (senior) or second (junior) in rank in an examination for the degree of Bachelor of Arts.
Someone who supervises and monitors the setting and marking of examinations by different people to ensure consistency of standards.
A mechanical arrangement for regulating motion in a machine, or producing equality of effect.

definitions.definition: A summary, précis or epitome; an abridgement or abstract.

definitions.definition: Occupying a small distance, area or spatial extent; short.

definitions.definition: Rife; common; prevalent.
Briefly.
Soon; quickly.

definitions.definition: Affected with or suffering from rickets.
A small river; a large creek; a body of moving water confined by banks.

definitions.definition: A possible one.

definitions.definition: Apparently valid, likely, plausible.

A physical wire or cable connection; landline

definitions.definition: Belonging to a hardline

A gradual change in color. A color gradient; gradation.

An artificial replacement of one or more teeth
(often in the plural) a complete replacement of all teeth in a mouth
An earthenware container, either with two or no handles, for holding oil, water, wine, etc., or used for burial.
A small, approximately cylindrical container, normally made of clay or glass, for holding fruit, preserves, etc., or for ornamental purposes.
A jar and its contents; as much as fills such a container; a jarful.
To preserve (food) in a jar.
A closely woven fabric (originally of silk, now also of cotton or man-made fibres) with a thick short pile on one side.
Very fine fur, including the skin and fur on a deer's antlers.
A female chinchilla; a sow.
The drug dextromethorphan.
Money acquired by gambling.
To cover with velvet or with a covering of a similar texture.
To coat raw meat in starch, then in oil, preparatory to frying.
To remove the velvet from a deer's antlers.
To soften; to mitigate.
(of a cat's claws) to retract.
Made of velvet.
Soft and delicate, like velvet; velvety.
Peaceful, carried out without violence; especially as pertaining to the peaceful breakup of Czechoslovakia.
The act of receiving, or the fact of having been received.
definitions.example: A balance payable on receipt of the goods.

definitions.definition: The fact of having received a blow, injury etc.
(in the plural) A quantity or amount received; takings.
definitions.example: This weekend's receipts alone cover our costs to mount the production!

definitions.definition: A written acknowledgment that a specified article or sum of money has been received.
(usually in the plural) (A piece of) evidence, documentation, etc. to prove one's past actions, accomplishments, etc.
(usually in the plural, by extension) (A piece of) evidence (e.g. documentation or screen captures) of past wrongdoing or problematic behavior or statements.
(archaic in New England and rural US since end of 20th century, elsewhere since middle of 20th century) A recipe, instructions, prescription.
A receptacle.
A revenue office.
Reception, as an act of hospitality.
Capability of receiving; capacity.
A recess; a retired place.
To give or write a receipt (for something).
definitions.example: to receipt delivered goods

definitions.definition: To put a receipt on, as by writing or stamping; to mark a bill as having been paid.
definitions.example: to receipt a bill

definitions.definition: A telecommunication device (originally mechanical, and now electronic) used for two-way talking with another person (now often shortened to phone).
The game of Chinese whispers.
To (attempt to) contact someone using the telephone.
To convey (a message) by telephoning.
Either of the two orifices located on the nose (or on the beak of a bird); used as a passage for air and other gases to travel the nasal passages.
A large Arctic marine mammal related to seals and having long tusks, tough, wrinkled skin, and four flippers, Odobenus rosmarus.
An assessment, such as an annual personnel performance review used as the basis for a salary increase or bonus, or a summary of a particular situation.
A completion of a mathematical operation; a valuation.
Determination of the value of a variable or expression.
A mythological undead creature said to feed on the blood of the living.
A person with the medical condition systemic lupus erythematosus, colloquially known as vampirism, with effects such as photosensitivity and brownish-red stained teeth.
A blood-sucking bat; vampire bat (Desmodus rotundus)
A person who drains one's time, energy, money, etc.
A vamp: a seductive woman who exploits men.
A medical technician who works with patients' blood.
To drain of energy or resources.
